data.title: What's Going On With Apple Stock Tuesday?
data.description:
Apple Inc (NASDAQ:AAPL) shares are getting a boost on Tuesday after Wedbush reiterated its Outperform rating and $350 price target.

  • Apple stock is moving in positive territory. Why is AAPL stock trading higher?

AI Strategy Back In Focus

Wedbush analyst Dan Ives said the weakness in Apple shares over the past few weeks has been driven by renewed anxiety that some of the company's long‑promised AI upgrades may slip beyond the summer window. But the analyst firm pushed back on that idea, calling the sell-off “unwarranted” and noting that Apple is still preparing to release its upgraded AI architecture across iOS 26.4, iOS 26.5 and iOS 27 in September.

The firm pointed to several developments it believes the market is overlooking, including Apple's finalized partnership with Google Gemini and the addition of new outside AI leadership inside Apple Park. Wedbush said Apple views the revamped Siri and its underlying AI framework as one of the most important technology launches in its history, given the scale of its 2.5 billion‑device ecosystem.

Wedbush also argued that Wall Street continues to underestimate the financial upside of Apple's AI strategy. The firm estimates that AI monetization could add $75 to $100 per share over the next few years as Apple introduces new services and subscription layers tied to its upgraded personal assistant.

Apple's Role In The AI Cycle

Wedbush said last year's AI stumbles now set the stage for Apple to roll out its Siri AI platform in 2026. The firm noted that concerns about possible delays will continue to create swings in the stock until Apple shows it can deliver, but emphasized that the company remains one of its top tech picks.

Apple remains one of Wedbush's top tech picks and a core member of its IVES AI 30 list. The firm said the consumer AI cycle will ultimately run through Cupertino as Apple shifts from a spectator to a central player in the next phase of the AI boom.

What Do The Technicals Show?

Apple is currently positioned above key resistance levels, indicating bullish momentum in the market. Traders should pay attention to how this setup could lead to further upward movement.

The RSI is currently neutral, suggesting that Apple’s stock isn’t overbought or oversold at this time. This neutrality can indicate a potential for continued price action without immediate reversal signals. MACD is above its signal line, which points to bullish momentum for Apple’s stock, suggesting that the current trend may have strength.

Key support sits at $243.50, while resistance is found at $277.50. If the stock approaches these levels, traders should watch for potential reversals or breakouts that could signal a change in trend direction.

Over the last 12 months, Apple’s stock has gained 7.29%, reflecting a generally upward trajectory despite some volatility. This longer-term performance highlights the stock’s resilience and potential for continued growth.

AAPL Price Action: Apple shares were up 2.78% at $262.96 at the time of publication on Tuesday, according to Benzinga Pro.

Image: Andrey Bayda/Shutterstock

data.title: Woman, 25, Sold Car to Single Mom Neighbor for $1,500 Instead Of Giving It To Reckless Brother — Now Family Says She's Selfish And Banned From Dinners
data.description:
Saving for years to buy a new car is usually a quiet win. For one 25 year old woman, it turned into a family blowup that ended with her being banned from Sunday dinner.

The situation came from a Reddit post where she explained that after three years of working overtime, she finally saved enough to buy a new truck. That meant her old sedan, which she described as "barely worth maybe $3,000, but it runs perfectly fine," was no longer needed.

Her younger brother, 19, immediately asked for it. She said no. In the post, she wrote, "I worked hard for that car and i wasn't going to watch him destroy it in a month," pointing to the fact that he had already totaled one vehicle and damaged another since getting his license. He accused her of being greedy since she already had a new vehicle.

Don't Miss:

  • Missed Nvidia and Tesla? RAD Intel Could Be the Next AI Powerhouse — Just $0.85 a Share
  • Put professional stock research to work in a single ETF — explore Motley Fool Asset Management's factor-based funds.

The Sale That Set Everything Off

While she was cleaning the sedan, a neighbor asked if it was for sale. The neighbor, a single mom who needed transportation to get to work, offered to buy it. The woman said she sold it for $1,500 just to get it off her driveway quickly, and the deal was done the next day.

When her brother realized the car was gone, she wrote that "he flipped out." Not long after, her mother called, telling her she was selfish for selling it cheaply to a stranger when family needed help and saying she should have let her brother use the car to practice driving instead.

The fallout escalated quickly. She wrote that she is now banned from Sunday dinner until she apologizes, adding, "I need to know if I messed up here because my family is blowing up my phone right now."

Trending: Designed for investors with strong market convictions, REX Shares builds ETFs for income, leverage, and tactical positioning — explore the lineup.

Her Car, Her Choice

The Reddit responses focused less on the family argument and more on ownership. Many commenters pointed out that the car belonged to her and that the decision followed naturally from that. One commenter summed it up simply, writing, "Your car. Your choice." Another said that if the family felt strongly about the brother needing a vehicle, they could step in themselves rather than expecting her to give one away.

Others noted that the situation felt familiar, where something earned through years of work suddenly becomes a shared expectation once it is no longer needed. The idea that she should absorb the cost because she upgraded struck a nerve with readers who said family help should still be voluntary.

"Banned from Sunday dinner? Take the win," another wrote. The consensus was clear: family support is a gift, not a debt—and sometimes, missing a Sunday dinner is a small price to pay for standing your ground.

When Money and Family Collide

At its core, the disagreement wasn't really about the neighbor or even the price. It was about who gets to decide what happens to something someone worked years to afford. Selling the car helped someone who needed it while also closing the chapter on something she had paid for herself, but to her family it looked like a missed opportunity to help one of their own.

Moments like this tend to blur the line between generosity and obligation, especially when money or property is involved. For some people, talking through those decisions with a financial advisor helps separate emotional pressure from financial choices, particularly when family expectations enter the picture.

Ultimately, the truck is hers, the old car was hers, and the decision followed that same logic. Family opinions may stick around for a while, but the choice itself never really belonged to anyone else. It was her money, her car, and her call.

Image: Imagn

data.title: Software Stocks Drop, Silver Plunges 6% To $72: What's Moving Markets Tuesday?
data.description:
Following the Presidents' Day holiday, the new week on Wall Street opened with the same fears weighing on software and tech stocks, as investors continued to grapple with potential AI-driven disruption.

The iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF (NYSE:IGV) fell more than 2.7% in midday New York trading, marking its 11th decline in the past 15 sessions and pushing year-to-date losses to nearly 25%.

Broader equity indices were largely flat on the day, though the tech-heavy Nasdaq 100 underperformed, slipping 0.3%.

Strength emerged in financials, which rebounded after weeks of weakness, while consumer staples lagged.

Airbnb Inc. (NASDAQ:ABNB) added 3.7%, extending last week's post-earnings momentum.

Southwest Airlines Co. (NYSE:LUV) jumped more than 6% following a wave of analyst upgrades from Susquehanna and UBS.

Commodities moved broadly lower after Iran's foreign minister said Tehran had reached an understanding with the U.S. on key principles. Crude oil fell 1.3% to $62 per barrel, while gold dropped 2.5% to $4,850 per ounce.

Silver slid nearly 6% to $72.80 an ounce.

Crypto sentiment was also weak, with Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) falling more than 2% to $67,000.

data.title: Tesla Stock Slides Tuesday: What's Happening?
data.description:
Tesla Inc (NASDAQ:TSLA) shares are trading lower Tuesday afternoon as investors digest fresh labor and regulatory headlines from Europe and the United States. Here’s what investors need to know.

  • Tesla stock is trending lower. What’s driving TSLA stock lower?

German Union Escalates Legal Fight With Tesla

In Germany, metalworkers' union IG Metall filed a criminal defamation complaint against the manager of Tesla's factory outside Berlin, accusing him of spreading false claims tied to an alleged secret recording of a works-council meeting, according to Reuters.

The union is also seeking a court injunction to halt further remarks and is preparing a separate case over what it calls obstruction of union work at Tesla's flagship European plant, Reuters reported.

Robotaxi Crash Record Raises Safety Questions

In the U.S., new federal incident filings, as compiled by EV outlet Electrek, show five additional crashes involving Tesla's supervised "Robotaxi" fleet in Austin in January 2026, lifting the total to 14 since mid-2025.

According to Electrek's analysis of Tesla's own mileage disclosures, that works out to roughly one crash every 57,000 miles, several times worse than the accident frequency Tesla itself cites for human drivers.

Prominent Investor Slams Tesla Brand Strategy

Adding to the negative tone, investor Ross Gerber said Tesla's brand has turned "negative" as CEO Elon Musk shifts focus from mass-market EVs toward autonomy and robotics.

He argued the company might sell more vehicles if it rebranded or even sold its EV business to rival Rivian Automotive Inc (NASDAQ:RIVN), underscoring mounting concern over strategy and perception.

Why This News Matters For TSLA

Together, labor unrest in Germany, a worsening Robotaxi safety record and public criticism of the brand amplify questions about execution risk, regulatory exposure and long-term demand. Those pressures can erode confidence in Tesla's ability to justify a premium valuation, helping explain why TSLA is under selling pressure Tuesday.

Tesla Faces Technical Headwinds

Tesla is currently trading 4.3% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 6.1% below its 100-day SMA, indicating short-term weakness while the longer-term trend remains intact.

Shares have increased 14.49% over the past 12 months and are currently positioned closer to their 52-week highs than lows, suggesting some resilience despite today’s decline.

The RSI is at a neutral level, indicating that the stock is neither overbought nor oversold. Meanwhile, MACD is below its signal line, suggesting bearish pressure on the stock.

The combination of neutral RSI and bearish MACD indicates mixed momentum, reflecting uncertainty in the stock’s near-term direction.

  • Key Resistance: $452.50
  • Key Support: $387.50

Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for Tesla, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Value: 3.15 — Trading at a steep premium relative to peers.
  • Quality: 63.78 — Balance sheet remains healthy.
  • Momentum: 75.16 — Stock is outperforming the broader market.

The Verdict: Tesla’s Benzinga Edge signal reveals a classic ‘High-Flyer’ setup. While the Momentum (75.16) confirms the strong trend, the Value (3.15) score warns that the stock is priced for perfection—investors should ride the trend but use tight stop-losses.

TSLA Price Action: Tesla shares were down 3% at $404.90 at the time of publication on Tuesday, according to Benzinga Pro.

Image: courtesy of Tesla
